about_page.title: Paul van den Hout
about_page.subtitle: Your personal support for real estate investment in Spain
about_page.bio_1: Throughout the past 30 years, Paul van den Hout has been closely involved with multinational companies as a sales-and marketing manager. He has been living and working in Spain since 1989.
about_page.bio_2: Paul worked for various multinationals, in the Tourism, Food & Beverage, Leisure and Real Estate industry. This experience has served him well creating his 'Helpdesk'. Initially he was based in Madrid during six years and afterwards he moved to Barcelona, where he runs his office from the centre of the city.
about_page.bio_3: Since 2014 Paul is also collaborating with the international Business School ESADE, on Leadership- and Cultural Change programs for (inter)national students.
about_page.bio_4: Paul has a 'Master in Real Estate', and is Certified, Professional Coach (PCC) with the International Coaching Federation (ICF), has an international certification in Coaching by Values and he's also a certified Teamcoach by the prestigious institute Gestalt.
about_page.bio_5: Cofounder of the coaching platform 'LIVT, elevate your talent', and he recently published the book El Camino (The Path) supporting adolescents, with the difficult decision of choosing their future career.

advisory_page.label: Purchase Assistance
advisory_page.title: Purchase Assistance
advisory_page.subtitle: Your personal support for real estate investment in Spain
advisory_page.opening: On the international real estate purchasing market, there is a huge demand for professional support. The Spanish system operates completely different to what people are used to at home. There is no national overall platform or portal, and real estate could be offered by anyone, at even different prices. Therefore, it is highly recommendable to use your own specialist, when you decide to invest in Spain.
advisory_page.intro: We are specialized in that specific item of the purchase process, through our help desk. We accompany the client from scratch until he has finalized the complete purchase. This consists the property search, pre-selection of the visits, negotiations with the seller, preparation of all the documents (multi-lingual) and the assistance at the Deed. It's an A to Z service package and we guarantee a safe and sure operation!
advisory_page.step1_desc: We search for properties matching your specific requirements, budget, and preferences across our extensive network.
advisory_page.step2_desc: We organize and pre-select the most relevant properties for you to visit, saving you valuable time.
advisory_page.step3_desc: We negotiate with the seller on your behalf, leveraging our knowledge of the local market to get you the best deal.
advisory_page.step4_desc: We prepare all the necessary documents in multiple languages, ensuring everything is legally correct.
advisory_page.step5_desc: We accompany you to the notary and assist with the signing of the Deed, including power of attorney if needed.
advisory_page.need_p1: We all know the value of the internet. It's a fantastic 24x7 shop that offers everything. A great showcase! However in this ocean of property offers, it is difficult to select the right property. The information that is offered is many times incomplete and what about the legal situation?
advisory_page.need_p2: We believe that buying real estate is a serious matter and concerns a great financial effort. How often will you be buying a house abroad in your life? Once, twice? Most professionals on the market are paid by the seller of a property. Bringing your own specialist with you, who only handles on your behalf, is not a bad idea.
advisory_page.need_quote: Bringing your own specialist with you, who only handles on your behalf, is not a bad idea. Together you could make a purchase plan. With his or her experience you know the market better before you decide. That will save you time and money.
advisory_page.pricing_text: Instead of charging huge amounts upfront, we only ask for a small fee as a starting capital. The remaining balance of the agreed fee, will be on a "no cure, no pay" base, and would only be charged at the moment of the completion of the purchase. We are willing to take that risk, because we are convinced that you'll become a satisfied client!
advisory_page.closing_text: If you don't want to waste your precious time, we are pleased to prepare a tailor made offer. We'll defend your interests during the whole process and you'll have your personal advisor working for you.
advisory_page.testimonial_project: Owner of a beautiful apartment in Barcelona
advisory_page.testimonial_quote: We found the services of VDH through the internet. Within hours they sent us a complete report with the 'ins & outs' of the local market and a quote of the proposed services. The day afterwards we asked Paul to accompany us with our search for a small palace in the centre of Barcelona, as we had very little time. We found a unique apartment, in downtown BCN, did the negotiations together, and had a fantastic deal! With such an important decision, it felt very safe to have someone you could trust, especially if you live so far away.

insights_page.label: Insights
insights_page.title: Buying a Property in Spain
insights_page.subtitle: Practical information and insights for international buyers considering property in Spain.
insights_page.intro: Many times, people underestimate the difficulties of buying a home abroad. It's not just the language problem, but there are also many cultural differences, between 'northern and southern Europe'. We share our knowledge to help you make informed decisions.
insights_page.article1_text: The Spanish real estate market operates completely different from any other European market. Unlike countries with a national overall platform, Spain has no centralized MLS system. Properties are listed across hundreds of local agencies, making it extremely difficult for foreign buyers to get a complete overview. This is where a local specialist becomes invaluable — someone who knows the market, the agencies, and can pre-select the right properties for your needs.
insights_page.article2_text: When purchasing property in Spain, buyers should budget approximately 10-13% on top of the purchase price for taxes and fees. This includes the property transfer tax (ITP) of 6-10% depending on the region, notary fees, land registry fees, and legal costs. For new-build properties, VAT (IVA) of 10% applies instead of ITP. Having a clear picture of all costs upfront prevents surprises at closing.
insights_page.article3_text: Every foreign buyer needs a NIE (Número de Identificación de Extranjero) — a tax identification number required for any financial transaction in Spain. This includes buying property, opening a bank account, signing contracts, and connecting utilities. The application can be made at the Spanish consulate in your home country or at the local police station in Spain. We handle this process for our clients as part of our service package.
insights_page.article4_text: Most professionals on the market are paid by the seller of a property. Bringing your own specialist with you, who only handles on your behalf, is not a bad idea. Together you could make a purchase plan. With his or her experience you know the market better before you decide. That will save you time and money. How often will you be buying a house abroad in your life? Once, twice? We believe that buying real estate is a serious matter and concerns a great financial effort.
insights_page.cta_title: Ready to take the next step?
insights_page.cta_text: If you don't want to waste your precious time, we are pleased to prepare a tailor made offer.

market_page.label: The Spanish Market
market_page.title: The Spanish Property Market
market_page.subtitle: Understanding the real estate market in Spain — key insights from 30+ years of experience.
market_page.intro: The Spanish real estate market offers incredible opportunities for international investors. From the vibrant city of Barcelona to the stunning Costa Brava coastline and the charming Sitges area, Spain combines quality of life with solid investment potential.
market_page.region1_text: The vibrant capital of Catalonia offers everything from Gothic Quarter apartments to modern Eixample flats and exclusive Sarrià villas. Barcelona combines world-class culture, cuisine, and connectivity with a Mediterranean lifestyle that attracts buyers from around the globe.
market_page.region2_text: From Tossa de Mar to Palamós, the Costa Brava coastline offers dramatic cliffs, hidden coves, and charming fishing villages. Property options range from beachfront apartments to luxurious villas with private pools and stunning sea views.
market_page.region3_text: Just 35 minutes from Barcelona, Sitges offers a sophisticated coastal lifestyle with beautiful beaches, excellent restaurants, and a vibrant cultural scene. The surrounding hills provide spectacular villa plots with panoramic Mediterranean views.

privacy_page.title: Privacy Policy
privacy_page.intro: VDH Enterprises ("we", "us", "our") respects your privacy and is committed to protecting your personal data. This privacy policy explains how we collect, use and safeguard your information when you visit our website.
privacy_page.section1_text: We collect personal information you voluntarily provide when using our contact form: your name, email address and message content. We also collect standard web analytics data such as IP addresses, browser type, pages visited and time spent on the site.
privacy_page.section2_text: We use your information to respond to your enquiries about property advisory services, to improve our website experience, and to comply with legal obligations. We do not sell, rent or share your personal information with third parties for marketing purposes.
privacy_page.section3_text: We retain contact form submissions for the duration of our business relationship and as required by applicable law. You may request deletion of your personal data at any time by contacting us.
privacy_page.section4_text: Under the EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and Spanish data protection law (LOPDGDD), you have the right to access, rectify, delete, restrict processing of, and port your personal data. To exercise these rights, contact us at info@vdhenterprises.com.
privacy_page.section5_text: For questions about this privacy policy or your personal data, please contact: VDH Enterprises, Barcelona, Spain. Email: info@vdhenterprises.com. Phone: (+34) 609 321 308.

legal_page.title: Legal Notice
legal_page.intro: In compliance with Spanish Law 34/2002 on Information Society Services and Electronic Commerce (LSSI-CE), VDH Enterprises provides the following legal information.
legal_page.section1_text: Company: VDH Enterprises. Activity: Independent property advisory services for international buyers in Spain. Location: Barcelona, Spain. Contact: info@vdhenterprises.com | (+34) 609 321 308.
legal_page.section2_text: By accessing this website, you agree to these terms. The content is provided for informational purposes about our property advisory services. While we strive for accuracy, property market information may change and should not be considered financial or legal advice.
legal_page.section3_text: All content on this website, including text, images, design and logos, is the property of VDH Enterprises or its licensors and is protected by intellectual property laws. Reproduction without written permission is prohibited.
legal_page.section4_text: VDH Enterprises shall not be liable for any damages arising from the use of this website or from decisions made based on the information provided herein. For specific property advice, please contact us directly for a personal consultation.
legal_page.section5_text: This legal notice is governed by Spanish law. Any disputes shall be submitted to the courts of Barcelona, Spain.

cookies_page.title: Cookies Policy
cookies_page.intro: This website uses cookies to improve your browsing experience. Below we explain what cookies are, which ones we use, and how you can manage them.
cookies_page.section1_text: Cookies are small text files stored on your device when you visit a website. They help the site remember your preferences and understand how you interact with the content.
cookies_page.section2_text: Essential cookies: Required for the website to function properly. These cannot be disabled. Analytics cookies: We use privacy-focused analytics to understand how visitors use our site. These help us improve the website experience. No advertising or tracking cookies are used on this website.
cookies_page.section3_text: You can control and delete cookies through your browser settings. Please note that disabling essential cookies may affect website functionality. For more information on managing cookies, visit your browser's help documentation.
cookies_page.section4_text: For questions about our use of cookies, contact us at info@vdhenterprises.com.

blog_sitges.label: Location Guide
blog_sitges.title: Sitges — South of Barcelona
blog_sitges.intro: Just 35 minutes from Barcelona, Sitges offers a sophisticated coastal lifestyle with beautiful beaches, excellent restaurants, and a vibrant cultural scene. It has long been a favourite destination for international buyers.
blog_sitges.section1_text: Sitges is one of the most charming coastal towns on the Mediterranean. Known for its beautiful beaches, historic old town, and vibrant cultural life, it has long been a favourite destination for international buyers seeking the perfect balance between coastal tranquility and urban accessibility. The town's proximity to Barcelona — just 35 minutes by car or train — makes it an ideal location for those who want to enjoy a relaxed Mediterranean lifestyle without being far from a major European city.
blog_sitges.section2_text: The surrounding hills of Sitges provide spectacular villa plots with panoramic views of the Mediterranean Sea. Property options range from charming townhouses in the historic centre to modern beachfront apartments and luxurious hilltop villas. The area attracts a diverse international community, which has created a cosmopolitan atmosphere while maintaining its authentic Catalan character.
blog_sitges.section3_text: Sitges combines the best of both worlds: a peaceful coastal town with excellent amenities, cultural events throughout the year, and easy access to Barcelona's international airport. The local gastronomy scene is outstanding, the beaches are among the best near Barcelona, and the community is welcoming to international residents. For property investors, Sitges offers strong rental potential due to its popularity with both tourists and long-term residents.
blog_sitges.section4_text: VDH Enterprises has been advising international buyers in the Sitges area since 1995. We have deep knowledge of the local market, from the beachfront properties to the exclusive hillside villas. Our spectacular villa of 265m² close to Sitges is one of the many successful projects we have completed in this area.

blog_process.label: Buyer's Guide
blog_process.title: Purchasing a Property in Spain — Ins & Outs of the Process
blog_process.intro: The Spanish real estate market operates completely different from any other European market. Unlike countries with a national overall platform, Spain has no centralized MLS system. Properties are listed across hundreds of local agencies, making it difficult for foreign buyers to get a complete overview.
blog_process.section1_text: On the international real estate purchasing market, there is a huge demand for professional support. The Spanish system operates completely different to what people are used to at home. There is no national overall platform or portal, and real estate could be offered by anyone, at even different prices. Therefore, it is highly recommendable to use your own specialist, when you decide to invest in Spain.
blog_process.section2_title: The Purchase Process: Step by Step
blog_process.section2_text: We accompany the client from scratch until the complete purchase is finalized. This consists the property search, pre-selection of the visits, negotiations with the seller, preparation of all the documents (multi-lingual) and the assistance at the Deed. It's an A to Z service package and we guarantee a safe and sure operation. The process typically involves: property search, pre-selection of visits, negotiations, due diligence, preliminary contract (arras), and finally the deed at the notary.
blog_process.section3_text: When purchasing property in Spain, buyers should budget approximately 10-13% on top of the purchase price for taxes and fees. This includes the property transfer tax (ITP) of 6-10% depending on the region, notary fees, land registry fees, and legal costs. For new-build properties, VAT (IVA) of 10% applies instead of ITP. Having a clear picture of all costs upfront prevents surprises at closing.
blog_process.section4_text: Every foreign buyer needs a NIE (Número de Identificación de Extranjero) — a tax identification number required for any financial transaction in Spain. This includes buying property, opening a bank account, signing contracts, and connecting utilities. The application can be made at the Spanish consulate in your home country or at the local police station in Spain. We handle this process for our clients as part of our service package.
blog_process.section5_text: We all know the value of the internet. It's a fantastic 24x7 shop that offers everything. A great showcase! However in this ocean of property offers, it is difficult to select the right property. The information that is offered is many times incomplete and what about the legal situation? Most professionals on the market are paid by the seller. Bringing your own specialist with you, who only handles on your behalf, is not a bad idea. Together you could make a purchase plan. With his or her experience you know the market better before you decide. That will save you time and money.

blog_barcelona.label: Investment Guide
blog_barcelona.title: Reasons to Buy a Property in Barcelona — The Best Investment
blog_barcelona.intro: Barcelona consistently ranks among Europe's most desirable cities for property investment. With its Mediterranean climate, world-class culture, and strong rental market, the Catalan capital offers compelling reasons for international buyers.
blog_barcelona.section1_text: The vibrant capital of Catalonia offers everything from Gothic Quarter apartments to modern Eixample flats and exclusive Sarrià villas. Barcelona combines world-class culture, cuisine, and connectivity with a Mediterranean lifestyle that attracts buyers from around the globe. The city consistently ranks among Europe's top destinations for quality of life, making it an attractive choice for both residents and investors.
blog_barcelona.section2_text: With over 480,000 properties sold annually across Spain, the market is active and liquid. Barcelona in particular benefits from sustained international demand, excellent infrastructure, and a diverse economy that supports long-term property values. The city's status as a major European tech hub, combined with its tourism appeal, creates strong fundamentals for both capital appreciation and rental income.
blog_barcelona.section3_text: Barcelona's distinctive neighbourhoods each offer a unique character and lifestyle. The Eixample district features elegant modernist architecture and wide boulevards. The Born and Gothic Quarter provide historic charm. Barceloneta offers beachfront living. Sarrià-Sant Gervasi delivers quiet, family-friendly streets with proximity to international schools. Poblenou has emerged as the creative and tech district. Each area serves different investment profiles.
blog_barcelona.section4_text: Paul van den Hout has been living and working in Barcelona since 1995. From our office near the Sagrada Familia, we have guided countless international buyers to find their ideal property — from investment apartments in the centre to family homes in the quieter residential areas. Our 30+ years of local knowledge means we understand every neighbourhood, every street, and every opportunity.

blog_foreigners.label: Market Analysis
blog_foreigners.title: Where Do Foreigners Buy Property in Spain?
blog_foreigners.intro: Spain remains one of Europe's most attractive destinations for international property buyers. Understanding where foreign buyers concentrate their investments helps identify the most dynamic market areas.
blog_foreigners.section1_text: International buyers in Spain tend to concentrate in specific regions that offer a combination of lifestyle, infrastructure, and investment potential. The Mediterranean coast dominates, with Catalonia (Barcelona and Costa Brava), the Costa del Sol (Málaga), the Balearic Islands (Mallorca, Ibiza), and the Costa Blanca (Alicante) being the most popular areas. The Canary Islands also attract a significant number of foreign buyers, particularly from northern Europe.
blog_foreigners.section2_text: The Barcelona metropolitan area and the Costa Brava coastline represent one of the most diverse property markets in Spain. Barcelona offers urban apartments and international connectivity, while the Costa Brava — from Tossa de Mar to Palamós — provides dramatic coastal scenery, charming fishing villages, and luxurious villa developments. This combination of urban sophistication and coastal tranquility makes our region particularly attractive to international buyers.
blog_foreigners.section3_text: The profile of foreign buyers in Spain has diversified significantly. While historically dominated by British, German, and Scandinavian buyers, the market now attracts investors from the Americas, Asia, and the Middle East. Dutch buyers have consistently been among the most active in the Barcelona and Costa Brava regions, alongside Belgian, French, and increasingly, American purchasers.
blog_foreigners.section4_text: We understand what foreign investors are looking for. We speak 5+ languages and we are specialized in bridging cultural differences. We love out of the box thinking. With our 30+ years of experience in Barcelona and Costa Brava, we know exactly which areas offer the best opportunities for each type of buyer — whether they seek a holiday retreat, a permanent residence, or a pure investment.

blog_demand.label: Market Analysis
blog_demand.title: Is Foreign Demand for Spanish Holiday Homes Sustainable?
blog_demand.intro: Foreign demand for property in Spain has shown remarkable resilience through multiple economic cycles. After 30+ years of observing this market, we share our perspective on what drives sustainable demand.
blog_demand.section1_text: The Spanish property market has demonstrated remarkable recovery capacity. After the 2008-2014 correction, international demand returned strongly and has continued to grow. With over 480,000 properties sold annually, the market is fundamentally healthy. Foreign buyers consistently account for a significant share of transactions, particularly in coastal regions and major cities. This sustained interest reflects Spain's enduring appeal as a destination for lifestyle and investment.
blog_demand.section2_text: Several fundamental factors support ongoing international demand: Spain's climate and quality of life consistently rank among the best in Europe. The country offers excellent healthcare and infrastructure. Property prices remain competitive compared to other Mediterranean countries. Strong rental yields, particularly in Barcelona and coastal areas, make investment compelling. The growing remote work trend has further expanded the pool of potential buyers who can now live and work from Spain.
blog_demand.section3_text: While the long-term outlook remains positive, buyers should be aware of market dynamics. Regional regulations on short-term rentals are evolving. Currency fluctuations can affect purchasing power for non-Euro buyers. Tax regulations for foreign owners require proper planning. These challenges underscore the importance of professional guidance — understanding the regulatory landscape is as important as finding the right property.
blog_demand.section4_text: Having operated in the Spanish property market since 1995, we have witnessed multiple cycles and can provide context that pure market data cannot. We offer a large track record with purchase advise, real estate services, facilitating and coaching in Spain. VDH is a Spanish limited company, operating in Barcelona since 1995, offering all the necessary guarantees. Our experience tells us that well-advised purchases in prime locations have consistently proven to be sound investments.

blog_brexit.label: Market Analysis
blog_brexit.title: Brexit and the Consequences for the Spanish Market
blog_brexit.intro: The UK's departure from the European Union raised questions about the impact on British property buyers in Spain. Years later, we assess the real consequences and what has changed.
blog_brexit.section1_text: When Brexit was announced, British buyers were the single largest foreign nationality purchasing property in Spain. The immediate concerns focused on residency rights, healthcare access, currency fluctuation, and whether British citizens would face new barriers to property ownership. The pound's decline against the euro immediately affected purchasing power for British buyers.
blog_brexit.section2_text: British citizens can still buy property in Spain without restrictions. However, residency rules have changed: UK nationals now fall under the non-EU visa regime, which limits stays to 90 days within a 180-day period without a visa. Those wishing to live in Spain need to apply for residency permits. Healthcare access now requires private insurance for non-residents. These administrative changes have added complexity but have not prevented purchases.
blog_brexit.section3_text: The Spanish property market has proven adaptable. While British buyer numbers initially declined, other nationalities — particularly German, Dutch, Belgian, and American buyers — have increased their activity. The market has diversified its buyer base, which in many ways has made it more resilient. In Barcelona and Costa Brava, Dutch and Belgian buyers have been particularly active, continuing a trend that predates Brexit.
blog_brexit.section4_text: Spain remains an attractive destination for British buyers who are willing to navigate the new administrative requirements. For advice on purchasing property in Spain as a non-EU citizen, including visa requirements and residency options, we recommend seeking specialized guidance. Our team at VDH Enterprises can connect you with the right legal professionals and guide you through the entire process.

project_sitges_villa.title: Spectacular Villa of 265m²
project_sitges_villa.intro: A spectacular villa close to Sitges with pool and panoramic Mediterranean views. One of our signature advisory projects.
project_sitges_villa.section1_text: This spectacular villa of 265m², located close to Sitges, features stunning views of the Mediterranean Sea. The property includes a private swimming pool, spacious terraces, and high-quality finishes throughout. Its privileged location offers the perfect balance between privacy and accessibility to the charming coastal town of Sitges.
project_sitges_villa.section2_text: VDH Enterprises provided complete advisory services for this transaction, including property identification, market analysis, price negotiation, and full assistance through the purchase deed. Our deep knowledge of the Sitges area was instrumental in identifying this exceptional opportunity.

project_girona.title: Apartments in Girona Golf Club House
project_girona.intro: Modern apartments within the Girona Golf Club House development, combining golf lifestyle with quality Mediterranean living.
project_girona.section1_text: The Girona Golf Club House development offers modern apartments within a premium golf community setting. The properties feature contemporary design, quality kitchens, and access to the golf course and club facilities. Located in the Girona province, the development combines sport and leisure with the tranquil Catalan countryside.
project_girona.section2_text: VDH Enterprises provided advisory and transaction support for investors interested in this development project. Our involvement included market positioning analysis, buyer matching, and complete purchase assistance through to deed signing.

project_calonge.title: Villa in Calonge, Costa Brava
project_calonge.intro: A minimalistic villa on a generous private plot of 680m² with swimming pool in Calonge, one of the Costa Brava's most desirable residential areas.
project_calonge.section1_text: This minimalistic villa in Calonge sits on a private plot of 680m², featuring clean architectural lines, a private swimming pool, and carefully designed outdoor spaces. Calonge is one of the Costa Brava's most sought-after residential areas, offering proximity to beautiful beaches while maintaining a peaceful, village atmosphere.
project_calonge.section2_text: This project was part of our Costa Brava development portfolio. VDH Enterprises was involved in the complete project supervision, from the initial design phase through construction oversight to final delivery and sales to international buyers.

project_barcelona.title: Fabulous Apartment of 136m²
project_barcelona.intro: A fabulous apartment of 136m² in the heart of Barcelona's city centre. A prime example of our urban property advisory.
project_barcelona.section1_text: Located in the very centre of Barcelona, this fabulous apartment of 136m² represents the best of city living. The property combines generous living spaces with the charm of a central Barcelona location, offering proximity to the city's cultural landmarks, restaurants, and international connectivity.
project_barcelona.section2_text: VDH Enterprises guided the international buyer through the entire acquisition process. From initial property search — with a pre-selection of suitable properties across Barcelona's diverse neighbourhoods — through negotiations with the seller, all documentation, and final assistance at the notary for the signing of the deed.

project_costa_brava_1.title: Luxurious Property in Costa Brava
project_costa_brava_1.intro: A luxurious property in the heart of the Costa Brava coastline, showcasing the region's finest residential offerings.
project_costa_brava_1.section1_text: This luxurious property in Costa Brava exemplifies the quality of residential real estate available along this dramatic Mediterranean coastline. The Costa Brava — from Tossa de Mar to Palamós — offers dramatic cliffs, hidden coves, and charming fishing villages, with property options ranging from beachfront apartments to luxurious villas with private pools and stunning sea views.
project_costa_brava_1.section2_text: VDH Enterprises provided complete advisory services for this Costa Brava property transaction. Our extensive network of contacts across the region, combined with 30+ years of local market knowledge, enabled us to identify and secure this exceptional property for our international client.

project_costa_brava_2.title: Independent Villa on Plot of 750m²
project_costa_brava_2.intro: An independent villa on a generous plot of 750m² with private swimming pool in the heart of the Costa Brava.
project_costa_brava_2.section1_text: This independent villa sits on a spacious plot of 750m² in the Costa Brava region. The property features a private swimming pool, landscaped gardens, and generous indoor-outdoor living spaces that take full advantage of the Mediterranean climate. The location offers proximity to the Costa Brava's finest beaches while maintaining a private, residential character.
project_costa_brava_2.section2_text: VDH Enterprises was involved in both the advisory and project supervision aspects of this property. We provided complete guidance from the initial search phase through purchase negotiations, and subsequently oversaw renovation works to bring the property to the client's specifications.
